tagline: Drive-thru salads worth pulling over for.
elevator_pitch: FGS is a fast-casual, drive-thru salad chain with an intentionally tiny menu of six perfected salads. Think In-N-Out, but for fresh, restaurant-quality salads you can grab in under five minutes.
problem: There is no fast, convenient way to get a genuinely great salad. Your options are either soggy fast-food afterthoughts or sit-down restaurants with 45-minute waits. Health-conscious eaters on the go are stuck choosing between bad salads and no salads at all.
solution: FGS offers a curated menu of just six meticulously developed salads, each inspired by the best signature salads from top restaurants. With a streamlined drive-thru model and pre-prepped fresh ingredients, orders are assembled in under two minutes. It is the In-N-Out of salads: simple, fast, and consistently excellent.
target_market: Health-conscious professionals aged 25 to 45 in suburban and urban areas, road trippers looking for fresh alternatives to fast food, and lunchtime office workers in busy commercial districts.
revenue_model: Per-unit salad sales at $12 to $16 per salad with high margins due to limited menu and streamlined prep. Additional revenue from fresh-squeezed lemonades, seasonal soups, and branded dressings sold retail. Franchise fees once the model is proven across 3 to 5 company-owned locations.
key_features: Six-item curated menu of perfected, restaurant-quality salads
Drive-thru and mobile order pickup for under-five-minute service
Fresh daily prep with locally sourced greens and premium proteins
Eco-friendly oversized compostable bowls designed for on-the-go eating
competitive_advantage: The radically simple menu means faster prep, less food waste, and unmatched consistency. Unlike Sweetgreen or Chopt, FGS does not ask you to build anything. Every salad is a finished, tested recipe. The drive-thru format removes the biggest friction point in healthy fast food: having to park and go inside.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. fast-casual restaurant market is valued at over $100 billion, and the health-focused segment is growing at roughly 10% annually. The salad and healthy bowl category alone represents an estimated $8 to $10 billion TAM, with significant whitespace in drive-thru formats.
go_to_market: Launch with a single flagship location in a high-traffic suburban commercial corridor. Build buzz through local food influencer partnerships and a bold brand identity. Offer a free salad to every customer on opening week to generate word-of-mouth and repeat visits.
risks: Perishable inventory and food waste if demand forecasting is off during early operations
Consumer perception that salads are not worth paying $12 to $16 for at a drive-thru
Competition from established fast-casual chains adding drive-thru salad options
year_one_milestones: Open flagship drive-thru location and reach 300 orders per day within 6 months
Achieve average ticket of $15 with 65% gross margin on food costs
Begin site selection for second and third locations based on unit economics
startup_cost_range: $250,000 to $500,000 for a single drive-thru location including build-out, equipment, initial inventory, and working capital
